# bitHumanKit

> Swift Package for on-device voice + lip-synced avatar chat on
> Apple Silicon. Drop into a Mac/iPad/iPhone app and ship a local
> conversational AI experience. No network calls, no API keys.
> Source: https://github.com/bithuman-product/bithuman-kit
> Made by bitHuman: https://www.bithuman.ai

This file is for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex,
GitHub Copilot Chat). It's the single canonical bundle to ingest
when reasoning about how to integrate or extend bitHumanKit. The
companion file `llms-full.txt` has the full public API surface +
worked examples for deeper context.

## What bitHumanKit gives you

A `VoiceChat` actor that owns a live conversation: microphone,
on-device speech recognition, an on-device language model,
on-device voice synthesis, and — optionally — a real-time
lip-synced animated face at 25 FPS.

Everything is `import bitHumanKit` and Swift Concurrency–native.
SwiftUI views for the avatar, agent picker, voice picker, prompt
editor, and a 25 FPS frame pump are all included and `public`.

## Install

```swift
.package(url: "https://github.com/bithuman-product/bithuman-kit.git",
         from: "0.8.0")
```

Targets: `bitHumanKit` (library, cross-platform).
Platform requirements: macOS 26+, iPadOS 26+, iOS 26+. Apple Silicon
only.

## Minimum integration (5 minutes)

```swift
import bitHumanKit

var config = VoiceChatConfig()
config.localeIdentifier = "en-US"
config.systemPrompt = "You are a calm assistant. One sentence per turn."
config.voice = .preset("Aiden")  // or .clone(referenceAudio:transcript:)

let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
try await chat.start()
// Conversation is now live: bot listens, transcribes, replies via TTS.
// Press the spacebar / call chat.stop() to end.
```

## Adding the avatar

```swift
let weights = try await ExpressionWeights.ensureAvailable { phase in
    // Optional progress callback. `phase` is .verifying / .downloading /
    // .verifyingDownloaded / .ready. UI hooks: download progress + ETA.
}
config.avatar = AvatarConfig(modelPath: weights, portraitPath: portraitURL)
// ...then chat.start() as above.

let coordinator = AvatarCoordinator(chat: chat)
coordinator.bindToOrchestrator()

// SwiftUI host:
//   AvatarRendererView (UIView on iOS, NSView on macOS — both conform to AvatarFrameSink)
//   FramePump drives it at 25 FPS
//   AvatarRootView (macOS) or compose your own with the public views (iOS)
```

The first call to `ExpressionWeights.ensureAvailable()` downloads
the universal weights bundle (~1.6 GB) to a local cache directory.
Subsequent launches are instant — sha256 verified.

## Public API surface (canonical types)

Engine + chat:
- `VoiceChat` — top-level actor; one instance per conversation.
- `VoiceChatConfig` — locale, system prompt, voice, optional avatar.
- `AvatarConfig` — `modelPath: URL` + `portraitPath: URL`.
- `VoiceSelection` — `.preset(String)` or `.clone(referenceAudio: URL, transcript: String)`.
- `TextChat` — text-only variant; same config; no audio graph.

UI:
- `AvatarCoordinator` — `@MainActor ObservableObject`. Reactive UI
  state (`isReady`, `idlePrewarmReady`, `idlePrewarmProgress`,
  `prewarmPortraitURL`, `muteAgentDuringPrewarm`,
  `orchestratorState`, `currentAgentCode`, `currentVoicePreset`,
  `currentSystemPrompt`). Commands: `swapPortrait(url:)`,
  `applyAgent(_:)`, `setVoicePreset(_:)`, `setSystemPrompt(_:)`,
  `previewVoice(_:)`, `show*Picker()`.
- `FramePump` — 25 FPS producer-consumer. `init(bithuman:chat:window:coordinator:)`.
- `AvatarFrameSink` (protocol) — `func render(_ frame: CGImage)`.
- `AvatarRendererView` — UIView/NSView. Both conform to `AvatarFrameSink`.
- `AvatarRootView` (macOS) — drop-in SwiftUI tree; floating circle.
- `LoadingParticleField` — splash with optional progress arc + portrait underlay.
- `BootSplashView`, `DownloadProgressView`, `StateRing`, `StateLabel` — composable splash + status pieces.
- `AgentPickerView`, `VoicePickerView`, `PromptEditorView` — pickers.

Catalog:
- `AgentCatalog` — `Agent.all`, 8 bundled personas (Diego default).
- `AgentCatalog.thumbnailURL(for:)` — bundled portrait JPGs.
- `PortraitGallery` — 5 portrait presets.
- `VoiceCatalog` — preset metadata.
- `PromptTemplate` — 6 starter system prompts.

Hardware gate:
- `HardwareCheck.evaluate() -> DeviceCapability` — call at app
  launch on iOS. Returns `.supported` or `.unsupported(reason:)`.
- `UnsupportedDeviceView(reason: String)` — refusal screen.

Weights:
- `ExpressionWeights.ensureAvailable(progress:) async throws -> URL`
  — downloads + verifies the universal weights artifact.

LLM-style helpers:
- `composeLLMInstructions(_ prompt: String) -> String` — appends a
  TTS-friendly suffix (no emoji, no markdown, no symbols, no
  read-aloud URLs) to whatever prompt you pass. The library applies
  this internally; the helper is exposed for callers that need to
  preview the exact text the LLM sees.

Brand:
- `BrandColors.coral` etc. — palette for matching SwiftUI surfaces.

## Common code patterns

Agent swap (face + voice + prompt at once, the recommended path):

```swift
let agent = AgentCatalog.all.first { $0.code == "A29QAR4629" }!  // Diego
coordinator.applyAgent(agent)
// Coordinator handles the full swap: face encoding, voice change,
// system prompt update. The pre-chat warm-up splash auto-fires;
// previous agent's audio cuts off; new agent stays silent until
// the warm-up completes.
```

Custom portrait (drag-drop / PhotosPicker / file URL):

```swift
coordinator.swapPortrait(url: localFileURL)
// Swap is async; UI shows a spinner overlay while the engine
// processes the new portrait; falls back to idle motion when done.
```

Hot-swap voice without changing face:

```swift
coordinator.setVoicePreset("af_heart")
```

Hot-swap system prompt without changing face/voice:

```swift
coordinator.setSystemPrompt("You are a tax-coding tutor. Use plain words.")
// TTS-friendly suffix is appended automatically — don't double-bake.
```

Barge-in (user starts speaking while bot is mid-sentence): handled
automatically. To wire UI hooks:

```swift
chat.onBargeIn = { [weak pump] in pump?.buffer.flushSpeech() }
```

## Errors → fixes

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `WeightsError.checksumMismatch` | Cached weights file corrupt | Delete the local cache; rerun. |
| `Bithuman.Error.unsupportedHardware` | Pre-M3 Mac, ≤16 GB iPad, sub-A18-Pro iPhone | Show `UnsupportedDeviceView`; gate via `HardwareCheck.evaluate()` first. |
| Mic permission denied | First launch, user said no | `chat.start()` throws; show retry UI; `AVAudioSession.requestRecordPermission`. |
| Jetsam mid-conversation (iOS) | Missing `increased-memory-limit` entitlement | Add entitlement; request Apple approval (1–3 day turnaround). |
| Splash never fades | Engine warm-up stalled (rare) | `coordinator.framePump?.resetIdleCache()` to retry. |

## Build / test / dev

```sh
swift build                    # cross-platform library + executables
swift test                     # 23 unit tests, no models needed
./validate-ios.sh              # confirms iOS Simulator compatibility
```

## Hardware floor

- macOS: M3+ Apple Silicon, macOS 26 (Tahoe).
- iPadOS: iPad Pro M4+, ≥ 16 GB unified memory, iPadOS 26.
- iOS: iPhone 16 Pro+ (A18 Pro), iOS 26.

`HardwareCheck.evaluate()` gates at runtime; surface the resulting
`UnsupportedDeviceView` instead of letting the engine load on
under-spec hardware.

## Memory budget

| Phase | iPad M4/M5 | iPhone 16 Pro (projected) |
|---|---:|---:|
| Idle | ~3.5 GB | ~3 GB |
| Active speech turn | ~5.5 GB peak | ~4–4.5 GB peak |
| Engine boot (one-shot) | ~3 GB transient | ~2.5 GB transient |

## Licence

Apache-2.0 source code. Expression engine weights ship under
bitHuman's separate weight licence — see the licence file bundled
inside the downloaded weights artifact.

- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/bithuman-product/bithuman-kit/issues
- Email: hello@bithuman.ai
- Docs: https://docs.bithuman.ai

For the full API surface + extended worked examples, see
`llms-full.txt` in this repo.
